title: Employ the Principle of Least Privilege Including for Specific Security Functions and Privileged Accounts
description: employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts

checks.name: Privileged Accounts are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that all privileged local accounts are identified and enumerated including local Administrators group members and any accounts with elevated user rights assignments

checks.name: Access to Privileged Accounts is Authorized per Least Privilege
checks.purpose: Confirm that privileged account membership is scoped to named individuals with no cross-role conflicts and no broad principals granted administrative access

checks.name: Security Functions are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that security functions such as audit policy management, firewall configuration, and user rights assignments are identified and mapped to specific privileged roles

checks.name: Access to Security Functions is Authorized per Least Privilege
checks.purpose: Confirm that access to security functions is restricted to named privileged accounts and not accessible to standard users via Group Policy user rights assignments

checks.name: Privileged Accounts are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that all privileged AD accounts are identified including Domain Admins, Schema Admins, Enterprise Admins, and local administrator accounts

checks.name: Access to Privileged Accounts is Authorized per Least Privilege
checks.purpose: Confirm that privileged AD group membership is scoped to named individuals with no conflicting cross-role memberships that violate least privilege

checks.name: Security Functions are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that security functions such as audit policy, firewall management, and AD administrative roles are identified and mapped to specific privileged groups

checks.name: Access to Security Functions is Authorized per Least Privilege
checks.purpose: Confirm that access to security functions is restricted to named privileged AD groups and standard users are explicitly denied sensitive user rights via Group Policy

checks.name: Privileged Accounts are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that all privileged accounts are identified including sudo/wheel group members, UID 0 accounts, and any accounts with unrestricted sudoers entries

checks.name: Access to Privileged Accounts is Authorized per Least Privilege
checks.purpose: Confirm that privileged group membership is scoped to named individuals with no high-risk cross-group conflicts and no non-root UID 0 accounts exist

checks.name: Security Functions are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that security functions such as firewall management, audit log access, and SELinux/AppArmor administration are identified and mapped to named privileged accounts

checks.name: Access to Security Functions is Authorized per Least Privilege
checks.purpose: Confirm that access to security functions is restricted via scoped sudoers entries, sensitive group membership controls, and SELinux/AppArmor policy enforcement

checks.name: Privileged Accounts are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that all privileged accounts are identified including sudo group members, UID 0 accounts, and any accounts with unrestricted sudoers entries

checks.name: Access to Privileged Accounts is Authorized per Least Privilege
checks.purpose: Confirm that privileged group membership is scoped to named individuals with no high-risk cross-group conflicts and no non-root UID 0 accounts exist

checks.name: Security Functions are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that security functions such as firewall management, audit log access, and AppArmor administration are identified and mapped to named privileged accounts

checks.name: Access to Security Functions is Authorized per Least Privilege
checks.purpose: Confirm that access to security functions is restricted via scoped sudoers entries, sensitive group membership controls, and AppArmor policy enforcement
